ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) ProxyLogon-style w3wp.exe web shell drop & child process on Exchange
ucs.description: Hunts the iconic HAFNIUM tradecraft from Operation Exchange Marauder: post-auth-bypass exploitation of on-prem Exchange (ProxyLogon CVE-2021-26855/26857/26858/27065) where the IIS worker (w3wp.exe MSExchange* app pool) writes a .aspx/.ashx web shell (China Chopper, Awen, etc.) under OWA/ECP virtual directories, then spawns reconnaissance/credential-staging children (cmd, powershell, net, nltest, procdump). Also catches the recurring Silk Typhoon pivot via on-prem Exchange/SharePoint zero-days (CVE-2024-49138 class issues) when w3wp is the parent of LOLBin children.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_name) as child_process values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" (Processes.parent_process="*MSExchange*" OR Processes.parent_process="*Exchange Server*" OR Processes.parent_process="*ECP*" OR Processes.parent_process="*OWA*") (Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","nltest.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","reg.exe","procdump.exe","rundll32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","7z.exe","makecab.exe")) by host Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.user Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | append [| tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as path values(Filesystem.process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.process_name="w3wp.exe" (Filesystem.file_name="*.aspx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.ashx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.asmx") (Filesystem.file_path="*\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\aspnet_client\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\owa\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\ecp\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Exchange Server\\V15\\ClientAccess\\*") by host Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`] | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 7d;
let _exch_paths = dynamic([@"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\", @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\", @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\", @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\aspnet_client\", @"\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\", @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\"]);
let _suspicious_children = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","nltest.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","reg.exe","procdump.exe","rundll32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","7z.exe","makecab.exe","hostname.exe","tasklist.exe"]);
let WebShellChildren = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("MSExchange","Exchange Server","ECP","OWA")  // Exchange app pools only
    | where FileName in~ (_suspicious_children)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
              EventKind = "w3wp_child_process",
              Detail = ProcessCommandLine,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              FileName;
let WebShellWrites = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
    | where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx" or FileName endswith ".asmx" or FileName endswith ".asp"
    | where FolderPath has_any (_exch_paths)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName,
              AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              EventKind = "webshell_aspx_drop",
              Detail = strcat(FolderPath, FileName),
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              FileName;
union WebShellChildren, WebShellWrites
| summarize ChildOrWriteCount = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen  = max(Timestamp),
            EventKinds = make_set(EventKind),
            Samples   = make_set(Detail, 25)
            by DeviceName, AccountName
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let lookback = 7d;
let _exch_path_re = @"(?i)\\(inetpub\\wwwroot\\aspnet_client|FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\(owa|ecp|aspnet_client)\\auth|Exchange Server\\V15\\(ClientAccess|FrontEnd\\HttpProxy))\\";
let _suspicious_children = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","nltest.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","reg.exe","procdump.exe","rundll32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","7z.exe","makecab.exe","hostname.exe","tasklist.exe"]);
let ChildProc = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
    | extend ChildBin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where ChildBin in (_suspicious_children)
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              EventKind = "w3wp_child_process",
              Detail = CommandLine,
              ParentImage = ParentProcessName,
              ChildBin;
let IISWebShell = W3CIISLog
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
    | where csUriStem matches regex _exch_path_re
    | where csUriStem matches regex @"(?i)\.(aspx?|ashx|asmx)$"
    | where csUriQuery has_any ("cmd=","exec=","whoami","powershell","shell","=Response.Write")
         or csMethod == "POST"
    | where scStatus == 200
    | summarize Hits = count(), Sample = any(strcat(csUriStem, "?", csUriQuery))
                by TimeGenerated = bin(TimeGenerated, 5m), Computer, csUriStem, cIP
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account = "",
              EventKind = "webshell_url_hit",
              Detail = strcat(csUriStem, " src=", cIP, " sample=", Sample),
              ParentImage = "",
              ChildBin = "";
union ChildProc, IISWebShell
| summarize Count = count(), FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated),
            EventKinds = make_set(EventKind), Samples = make_set(Detail, 25)
            by Computer, Account
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Exchange IIS Worker Spawns LOLBin or Drops .aspx Web Shell (HAFNIUM / Silk Typhoon)
id: 7c4b2f8d-9a14-4f3a-b6c1-2e9c4f1c8d11
status: experimental
description: Detects HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon ProxyLogon-style post-exploitation where the Exchange IIS worker (w3wp.exe) spawns command interpreters / recon LOLBins or writes a web shell into an OWA/ECP virtual directory.
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/09
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
      - '\procdump.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\7z.exe'
      - '\makecab.exe'
      - '\hostname.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
  filter_machine:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and not filter_machine
fields:
  - ComputerName
  - User
  - ParentImage
  - ParentCommandLine
  - Image
  - CommandLine
falsepositives:
  - Exchange cumulative-update / setup scripts (filter on known maintenance windows)
  - Legitimate Exchange management agent invocations
level: high
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.t1059.001
  - detection.threat_hunting
  - g0125
ucs.rationale: Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM is THE actor associated with mass exploitation of on-prem Exchange (Operation Exchange Marauder, March 2021) and re-uses the same playbook on every fresh Exchange/SharePoint n-day they pick up. The signature is unmistakable: w3wp.exe (Exchange app pool identity) is the parent of cmd/powershell/recon LOLBins, AND/OR the same w3wp.exe writes .aspx files into OWA/ECP virtual di
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma tag must start with namespace separated with dot from remaining tag.

ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) LSASS credential dump via comsvcs.dll MiniDump / procdump after Exchange compromise
ucs.description: Catches the post-exploitation credential-access stage HAFNIUM has used repeatedly: dumping LSASS memory via the built-in comsvcs.dll MiniDump export (rundll32 comsvcs.dll #24 / MiniDump) or via Sysinternals procdump -ma lsass, typically launched from an Exchange-hosting server within minutes-to-hours of web shell installation. This pairs with UC1 (web shell) to flag the kill-chain pivot from initial access into credential theft — the moment HAFNIUM moves from Exchange to AD.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where ((Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll*" (Processes.process="*MiniDump*" OR Processes.process="*#24*")) OR (Processes.process_name="procdump*.exe" Processes.process="*lsass*" (Processes.process="*-ma*" OR Processes.process="*-mp*")) OR (Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") (Processes.process="*MiniDumpWriteDump*" OR Processes.process="*Out-Minidump*" OR Processes.process="*-ProcessName lsass*"))) by host Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.user Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 7d;
// HAFNIUM-favoured LSASS dump tradecraft
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where
    // 1) comsvcs.dll MiniDump (signed, on-disk, no tooling needed)
    (FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
        and ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs.dll"
        and (ProcessCommandLine has "MiniDump" or ProcessCommandLine has "#24"))
    or
    // 2) procdump (often renamed but command-line gives it away)
    (ProcessCommandLine has_all ("lsass")
        and ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-(ma|mp|mm)(\s|$)")
    or
    // 3) PowerShell reflection / Out-Minidump
    (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
        and (ProcessCommandLine has "MiniDumpWriteDump"
          or ProcessCommandLine has "Out-Minidump"
          or (ProcessCommandLine has "lsass" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("GetProcessById","OpenProcess","WriteAllBytes"))))
| extend Method = case(
    ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs.dll", "comsvcs_minidump",
    FileName startswith "procdump" or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-(ma|mp|mm)\b", "procdump",
    "powershell_reflection")
// Enrich: was this host running Exchange (UC1 pivot)?
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("MSExchange","Exchange Server")
    | summarize ExchangeWebShellSeen = max(Timestamp) by DeviceId
) on DeviceId
| extend ChainedWithExchangeWebShell = isnotnull(ExchangeWebShellSeen)
         and datetime_diff('hour', Timestamp, ExchangeWebShellSeen) between (0 .. 168)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, Method, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName, ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChainedWithExchangeWebShell, ExchangeWebShellSeen
| order by ChainedWithExchangeWebShell desc, Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let lookback = 7d;
let LsassDumps = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend Bin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
             Cmd = tolower(CommandLine)
    | where
        (Bin == "rundll32.exe" and Cmd has "comsvcs.dll" and (Cmd has "minidump" or Cmd has "#24"))
        or (Cmd has "lsass" and Cmd matches regex @"(?i)\s-(ma|mp|mm)(\s|$)")
        or (Bin in ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") and (Cmd has "minidumpwritedump" or Cmd has "out-minidump" or (Cmd has "lsass" and Cmd has_any ("getprocessbyname","openprocess"))))
    | extend Method = case(
        Cmd has "comsvcs.dll", "comsvcs_minidump",
        Cmd matches regex @"(?i)\s-(ma|mp|mm)\b", "procdump",
        "powershell_reflection")
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Method, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
              ParentImage = ParentProcessName;
let ExchangeHosts = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
    | where CommandLine has_any ("MSExchange","Exchange Server") or ParentProcessName has "Exchange"
    | summarize ExchangeWebShellSeen = max(TimeGenerated) by Computer;
LsassDumps
| join kind=leftouter ExchangeHosts on Computer
| extend ChainedWithExchangeWebShell = isnotnull(ExchangeWebShellSeen)
         and datetime_diff('hour', TimeGenerated, ExchangeWebShellSeen) between (0 .. 168)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Method, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentImage, ChainedWithExchangeWebShell, ExchangeWebShellSeen
| order by ChainedWithExchangeWebShell desc, TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: LSASS Credential Dump via comsvcs.dll MiniDump or procdump (Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM)
id: 4d7a91c5-3b62-4c2f-a8d7-9f5b1e2c4a90
status: experimental
description: HAFNIUM / Silk Typhoon post-exploitation pattern — LSASS memory dumped via signed comsvcs.dll MiniDump export, procdump -ma lsass, or PowerShell MiniDumpWriteDump reflection. Frequently observed within hours of an Exchange web shell drop (UC paired with HAFNIUM web shell detection).
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/09
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1003/001/
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  comsvcs:
    Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'comsvcs.dll'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'MiniDump'
      - '#24'
  procdump:
    CommandLine|contains: 'lsass'
    CommandLine|re: '(?i)\s-(ma|mp|mm)(\s|$)'
  ps_reflection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'MiniDumpWriteDump'
      - 'Out-Minidump'
  filter_machine:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: (comsvcs or procdump or ps_reflection) and not filter_machine
fields:
  - ComputerName
  - User
  - Image
  - CommandLine
  - ParentImage
falsepositives:
  - Sanctioned IR / red-team operations using comsvcs.dll or procdump
  - Microsoft support diagnostic dumps (rare, scope by host + scheduled window)
level: critical
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1003.001
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.003
  - g0125
ucs.rationale: MSTIC's HAFNIUM disclosure and DFIR Report write-ups of follow-on Silk Typhoon intrusions repeatedly call out comsvcs.dll MiniDump and procdump-against-lsass as the credential-access step the actor reaches for after dropping the China-Chopper-style web shell. The technique is rare in benign environments — comsvcs.dll #24 / MiniDump has essentially zero legitimate command-line invocations outside r
